article_title: UC San Diego Engineers CRISPR Gene Drive That Spreads Through Bacteria to Strip Away Antibiotic Resistance

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: UC San Diego Engineers CRISPR Gene Drive That Spreads Through Bacteria to Strip Away Antibiotic Resistance
content_preview.summary: Researchers at the University of California San Diego have developed pPro-MobV, a second-generation CRISPR-based gene drive that spreads through bacterial populations via conjugal transfer to disable antibiotic resistance genes, demonstrating effectiveness even within biofilms.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Researchers at the University of California San Diego have developed a CRISPR-based genetic tool that can spread through bacterial communities and disable the genes responsible for antibiotic resistance, according to findings [published in npj Antimicrobials and Resistance](https://www.nature.com/articles/s44259-026-00181-z) in February 2026. The system, called pPro-MobV, represents a second-generation approach that adapts gene drive technology -- previously used in insect populatio...

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear biotech reporting with accessible explanation of gene drive mechanics. Good balance between significance and limitations.
editor_notes.source_verification: UC San Diego (fetched): Confirms pPro-MobV, conjugal transfer, biofilm function, reversibility, Bier/Meyer, npj Antimicrobials and Resistance, bacteriophage delivery. ScienceDaily (fetched): Confirms same facts, Bier quote on 'neutralize AR in a large target population,' 10M deaths/year by 2050 projection. Phys.org and Nature paper listed but verified through overlapping content.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. Technical details (pPro-MobV, conjugal transfer, biofilm penetration, reversibility) confirmed across sources.
editor_notes.originality: Prior CRISPR articles cover Intellia/FDA clinical hold — different topic entirely. This is unique.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong biotech science news. Ready for publication.
